title: Why IoT at Scale Struggles in Africa – And What Comes Next
author: peter.walsh@commscloud.com
excerpt: Why IoT at Scale in Africa Struggles — and how CommsCloud’s Cloud Connect SIM cards solve cross-border connectivity challenges across Southern Africa.
content_text: The Harsh Reality: Why IoT at Scale Struggles in Africa Despite bold visions of connected devices and smart cities, IoT at scale deployments in Africa stall at the starting line. Here's why: Blackouts and Network Gaps Mobile operators often prioritise consumer traffic, leaving IoT devices scrambling for signal — especially across borders. When your solution relies on real-time video, tracking, or voice, even a brief dropout can be a serious operational risk. Roaming Costs That Drain Budgets Traditional roaming SIMs aren’t built for affordability or scale. Businesses often end up paying premium rates for inconsistent performance — a lose-lose situation. No Visibility or Root Cause Diagnosis When something breaks, it’s anyone’s guess: is it the SIM? The device? The network? Local support teams often lack the tools to diagnose problems across the full tech stack. Fragmented Support Ecosystems From firmware to dashboards to network configurations to local mobile operator dashboards, Africa

meta._elementor_data:
[{"id":"c17d4a7","elType":"section","settings":{"pa_condition_repeater":[]},"elements":[{"id":"5cf365c","elType":"column","settings":{"_column_size":100,"_inline_size":null,"pa_condition_repeater":[]},"elements":[{"id":"660ba02","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

The Harsh Reality: Why IoT at Scale Struggles in Africa<\/strong><\/h2>

Despite bold visions of connected devices and smart cities, IoT at scale deployments in Africa stall at the starting line. Here's why:<\/p>

Blackouts and Network Gaps<\/strong><\/p>

Mobile operators often prioritise consumer traffic, leaving IoT devices scrambling for signal \u2014 especially across borders. When your solution relies on real-time video, tracking, or voice, even a brief dropout can be a serious operational risk.<\/p>

Roaming Costs That Drain Budgets<\/strong><\/p>

Traditional roaming SIMs aren\u2019t built for affordability or scale. Businesses often end up paying premium rates for inconsistent performance \u2014 a lose-lose situation.<\/p>

No Visibility or Root Cause Diagnosis<\/strong><\/p>

When something breaks, it\u2019s anyone\u2019s guess: is it the SIM? The device? The network? Local support teams often lack the tools to diagnose problems across the full tech stack.<\/p>

Fragmented Support Ecosystems<\/strong><\/p>

From firmware to dashboards to network configurations to local mobile operator dashboards, African IoT projects involve a complex web of components. Without an integrated support partner, issues escalate \u2014 and uptime suffers.<\/p>

Single CORE Network\u00a0<\/strong><\/p>

Most IoT connectivity providers operate a single core network with multiple IMSIs. High resiliency and redundancy require a multi-IMSI profile solution to operate on geo-redundant CORE Networks. Ensuring that when the inevitable outages come, your devices stay up.\u00a0\u00a0<\/p>

Say Goodbye to IoT Frustration in Africa<\/strong><\/p>

IoT connectivity solutions in Africa are bursting with potential \u2014 but also fraught with failure. Imagine a fleet vehicle crossing the border, its camera feed and tracking devices dropping connections just as it enters a high-risk zone. Or a push-to-talk (PTT) radio that loses signal mid-emergency. For too long, businesses have been forced to accept these failures as part of doing business on the continent.<\/p>

But what if the problem wasn\u2019t Africa itself \u2014 but the outdated SIM cards, network limitations, and support structures behind them?<\/p>

At CommsCloud, we\u2019ve worked alongside African IoT Solution Integrators, OEM engineers, and IoT integrators who\u2019ve faced these challenges firsthand. That\u2019s why we built Cloud Connect: the SIM card designed not just to survive Africa\u2019s complexity, but to conquer it.<\/p>

Let\u2019s explore why IoT often fails in Africa \u2014 and what CommsCloud is doing to rewrite that story.<\/p>

The CommsCloud Solution: Built for African Conditions<\/strong><\/h2>

Cloud Connect isn\u2019t just another IoT SIM \u2014 it\u2019s the result of asking, \u201cWhat would it take to actually make IoT work in Africa?\u201d<\/em><\/p>

Autonomous Multi-Network Switching<\/strong><\/p>

Our Cloud Connect SIMs automatically switch between networks across countries. That means no more fiddling at border posts or dealing with dropped coverage. Your fleet stays connected \u2014 period.<\/p>

Carrier-Grade Visibility and Control<\/strong><\/p>

With a single, centralised platform, you can monitor, diagnose, and manage every device \u2014 from Zambia to South Africa to DRC. See SIMs, devices, and networks in one place, in real time.<\/p>

Uptime-First Design<\/strong><\/p>

We\u2019ve embedded resilience at every level: no roaming gaps, local breakout points, and support for high-data use cases like dashcams, remote sensors, and PTT radios.<\/p>

IoT-Savvy Human Support<\/strong><\/p>

Need help troubleshooting across hardware, firmware, and SIMs? You\u2019ll talk to someone who understands IoT, not someone reading from a generic call centre script.<\/p>

Real Market Needs, Real Solutions<\/strong><\/h2>

Africa isn\u2019t behind \u2014 it\u2019s leapfrogging. Smart cities, connected agriculture, fleet automation, and cross-border logistics are all accelerating. But they need infrastructure that matches their ambition.<\/p>

Cloud Connect SIM cards answers that call:<\/strong><\/p>

  • Fleet Safety:<\/strong> Real-time dashcam footage, cross-border tracking, and tamper-proof alerts.<\/li>
  • Security Operations:<\/strong> Always-on push-to-talk radios with low latency and SIM-based VPNs.<\/li>
  • OEM Deployments:<\/strong> One SIM for all devices \u2014 no integration chaos or SKU juggling.<\/li>
  • Remote Monitoring:<\/strong> From boreholes to solar farms, get reliable telemetry without expensive setup.<\/li><\/ul>

    5 Reasons Why IoT at Scale Succeeds with Cloud Connect SIM Cards<\/strong><\/p>

    1. One SIM, Multiple Networks:<\/strong> Forget managing different SIMs for different countries.<\/li>
    2. Real-Time Diagnostics:<\/strong> See exactly what\u2019s happening \u2014 no more guesswork.<\/li>
    3. Zero-Roaming Architecture:<\/strong> Eliminate hidden costs that erode your margins.<\/li>
    4. 24\/7 Support:<\/strong> Local expertise with global reach, tailored to your setup.<\/li>
    5. Faster Time-to-Scale:<\/strong> Simplify trials and deployments with pre-tested bundles.<\/li><\/ol>

      The Future of African IoT Starts Here<\/strong><\/h2>

      IoT doesn\u2019t fail in Africa because Africa is too hard. It fails because providers haven\u2019t taken the time to understand African business needs. At CommsCloud, we\u2019ve done the work \u2014 from local network integrations to regional compliance and language support.<\/p>

      Cloud Connect is the SIM that finally lets your devices roam without compromise, stream without dropout, and scale without fear.<\/p>

      Looking for a multi-IMSI solution that truly delivers on its promise? Reach out to CommsCloud IoT Connectivity Solutions | <\/strong>Empowering Your Business to Connect, Grow, and Thrive \u2013 Contact Us <\/strong>| +27 21 551 5526 or\u00a0\u00a0Get in Touch<\/a><\/strong>and follow our journey across Africa with the social media links below.<\/p>","pa_condition_repeater":[]},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":false}],"isInner":false}]

title: How to Choose IoT SIM cards for Scale
author: peter.walsh@commscloud.com
excerpt: Not all SIMs are equal. Learn how to choose the right IoT SIM card for Africa’s toughest use cases — and why Cloud Connect leads.
content_text: Not All IoT SIM Cards Are Designed for Scale Did your SIM provider design IoT SIM cards for scale? You’ve invested in high-performance in-vehicle cameras, tracking devices, and push-to-talk radios — yet your SIMs keep dropping off the grid. Sound familiar? The wrong SIM card can quietly undermine even the best hardware. And for IoT deployments in Southern Africa — where devices cross borders, networks vary, and roaming costs pile up — the risk is even greater. At CommsCloud, we’ve built our Cloud Connect SIMs to solve exactly these problems. If you're scaling IoT in Africa, here’s what to look for in a SIM — and why it matters. Why the Wrong SIM Costs More Than You Think A bad SIM doesn’t just cause downtime. It disrupts safety systems, breaks SLA commitments, and kills visibility when needed. The fallout? Lost video footage during critical driving events Dropped PTT calls when seconds matter Vehicles gone dark , triggering insurance disputes Wasted field time from repeated site visits

meta._elementor_data:
[{"id":"6fbd211","elType":"section","settings":{"pa_condition_repeater":[]},"elements":[{"id":"05f054c","elType":"column","settings":{"_column_size":100,"_inline_size":null,"pa_condition_repeater":[]},"elements":[{"id":"1e8d9d8","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

Not All IoT SIM Cards Are Designed for Scale<\/strong><\/h2>

Did your SIM provider design IoT SIM cards for scale?<\/strong>
You\u2019ve invested in high-performance in-vehicle cameras, tracking devices, and push-to-talk radios \u2014 yet your SIMs keep dropping off the grid. Sound familiar?<\/p>

The wrong SIM card can quietly undermine even the best hardware. And for IoT deployments in Southern Africa \u2014 where devices cross borders, networks vary, and roaming costs pile up \u2014 the risk is even greater.<\/p>

At CommsCloud, we\u2019ve built our Cloud Connect SIMs to solve exactly these problems.<\/p>

If you're scaling IoT in Africa, here\u2019s what to look for in a SIM \u2014 and why it matters.<\/p>

Why the Wrong SIM Costs More Than You Think<\/strong><\/h2>

A bad SIM doesn\u2019t just cause downtime. It disrupts safety systems, breaks SLA commitments, and kills visibility when needed. The fallout?<\/p>

  • Lost video footage<\/strong> during critical driving events<\/li>
  • Dropped PTT calls<\/strong> when seconds matter<\/li>
  • Vehicles gone dark<\/strong>, triggering insurance disputes<\/li>
  • Wasted field time<\/strong> from repeated site visits<\/li>
  • Endless<\/strong> Ticket logging and support loops\u00a0<\/li><\/ul>

    Your connectivity should be silent and seamless, not a source of risk.<\/p>

    6 Non-Negotiables to Choose IoT SIM Cards for Scale\u00a0<\/strong><\/h2>
    1. \u2705 Autonomous Multi-Network Switching<\/strong>
      Your SIM must utilise autonomous failover \u2014 no manual switching. Static roaming profiles are obsolete.
      CommsCloud IoT Infrastructure:<\/strong><\/em> Cloud Connect SIMs switch autonomously across leading mobile networks as well as across our Core Networks, each with its own IMSI,\u00a0 across Southern Africa, and beyond.<\/li>
    2. \u2705 Real-Time Visibility & Remote Management<\/strong>
      Can you diagnose issues remotely? Force a reconnect? Push device updates? Do you have uniform visibility and SIM management, alerts, and reporting no matter which mobile network, region, or continent your device is in? If not, you\u2019re flying blind.
      What We Offer:<\/strong><\/em> Our connectivity management portal shows every SIM\u2019s live status, packet activity, and signalling events in the same way, on more than 14 operator profiles in 217 countries for 600+ mobile operators globally \u2014 so you can fix problems fast.<\/li>
    3. \u2705 Resilience Against Roaming Dropouts<\/strong>
      Legacy roaming SIMs often fail in Africa\u2019s complex environments.
      Built for Africa:<\/strong><\/em> Our SIMs utilise multi-IMSI operator IMSI profiles on a single UICC SIM card, with local breakout via regional PGWs and commercial agreements and solutions purpose-built for the challenges of the Southern African IoT landscape.<\/li>
    4. \u2705 One SKU Simplicity for OEMs<\/strong>
      Shipping products into 10 countries shouldn\u2019t mean 10 different SIM contracts.
      Simplify with Us:<\/strong><\/em> One SKU. All SIM types (2FF\/3FF\/4FF\/UICC\/eUICC\/iSIM\/Industrial grade). Certified with Teltonika, Ruptela, Galileosky, and more.<\/li>
    5. \u2705 Carrier-Grade Support from IoT Specialists<\/strong>
      You need escalation paths and people who understand your hardware. You cannot scale IoT using mobile operator consumer networks designed for mobile phones and data dongles.
      Real Support:<\/strong><\/em> Our business development, pre-sales, and support teams live and breathe IoT, offering expert, context-aware support when you need it.<\/li>
    6. \u2705 Ready for What\u2019s Next: 5G, NB-IoT & More<\/strong>
      If your SIM can\u2019t handle LTE-M, NB-IoT, or fixed IP for live video, your deployment can\u2019t evolve.
      Future-Proofed:<\/strong><\/em> Cloud Connect runs on top of floLIVE\u2019s<\/a> hyper-local global IoT infrastructure and supports next-gen IoT networks, private APN\/VPNs, and has 40+ localised PGW breakouts \u2014 built-in.<\/li><\/ol>

      Questions Every Fleet Manager Should Ask Before Their Next SIM Purchase<\/strong><\/h2>
      • Will this SIM roam seamlessly<\/strong> across Africa and the world?<\/li>
      • Can it handle data-heavy use cases<\/strong> like live dashcam uploads and push-to-talk solutions across Southern Africa?<\/li>
      • Will the business development, pre-sales, and support teams understand my devices<\/strong>, not just smartphones?<\/li>
      • Can I get early alerts<\/strong> to avoid downtime \u2014 not just respond to it?<\/li>
      • Do I have real-time insight<\/strong> with uniformity into what every SIM is doing?<\/li><\/ul>

        If the answer is \u201cno,\u201d it\u2019s time to upgrade.<\/p>

        Cloud Connect SIMs: Built for Africa\u2019s Real-World IoT<\/strong><\/h2>

        Whether you're a fleet operator, OEM, or systems integrator \u2014 Cloud Connect gives you:<\/p>

        • Control<\/strong> \u2013 Live SIM and device status, anytime<\/li>
        • Continuity<\/strong> \u2013 Resilient, borderless connectivity across networks<\/li>
        • Automation <\/strong>\u2013 Business Service Systems that support IoT and SIM applet technology that fails over autonomously<\/li>
        • Confidence<\/strong> \u2013 24\/7 IoT-smart business development, pre-sales and support teams backed by scalable global IoT infrastructure<\/li><\/ul>

          Your SIM Is the Foundation \u2014 Not a Footnote<\/h2>

          In Africa\u2019s fast-moving IoT landscape, the SIM you choose defines your uptime, your margins, and your ability to scale.<\/p>

          With CommsCloud\u2019s Cloud Connect SIMs, you get more than connectivity \u2014 you get a partner who understands your use case, your devices, and your region.<\/p>

          Test it for yourself: Try our 30-day Proof of Concept<\/strong>
          \u2705 5 SIMs
          \u2705 Zero paperwork, no contracts and low cost entry points
          \u2705 Full access to our platform and support<\/h2>

Follow our journey across Africa with the social media links below.<\/p>","pa_condition_repeater":[]},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":false}],"isInner":false}]

title: Inside CommsCloud’s CMP. Why Every IoT Provider Needs a Connectivity Management Portal.
author: peter.walsh@commscloud.com
excerpt:
In IoT, success doesn’t just come from the device or the data—it hinges on reliable, scalable, and controllable connectivity. Yet too often, IoT solution providers discover that managing thousands of SIMs, multiple networks, and complex deployments is harder than expected. Devices go dark, costs spiral, and troubleshooting becomes a guessing game.

That’s why every serious IoT provider needs a Connectivity Management Portal (CMP). The CommsCloud CMP—powered by floLIVE—has been built for Africa’s complexity and global IoT demands, giving providers the visibility, control, and flexibility they need to deliver resilient IoT at scale.

[{"id":"2ebf027","elType":"section","settings":{"pa_condition_repeater":[]},"elements":[{"id":"f6a1391","elType":"column","settings":{"_column_size":100,"_inline_size":null,"pa_condition_repeater":[]},"elements":[{"id":"ac12283","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

Every serious IoT provider needs a Connectivity Management Portal (CMP)<\/strong>. The CommsCloud CMP\u2014powered by floLIVE<\/a>\u2014has been designed and shaped to meet the complexity of Africa and the global demands of IoT, providing providers with the visibility, control, and flexibility they need to deliver resilient IoT at scale.<\/p>

In IoT, success doesn\u2019t just come from the device or the data\u2014it hinges on reliable, scalable, and controllable connectivity. Yet, too often, IoT solution providers discover that managing thousands of SIMs, multiple networks, and complex deployments is more challenging than expected. Devices go dark, costs spiral, and troubleshooting becomes a game of guesswork.<\/p>

Challenges IoT Providers Face Without a CMP<\/h2>

1. Blind Spots in Connectivity<\/strong>
Without a centralised view, IoT teams don\u2019t know when devices stop transmitting data, or why. Network issues, roaming blackouts, or misconfigured firmware remain invisible until customers complain.<\/p>

2. Rising Operational Costs<\/strong>
Manually monitoring SIMs and billing across multiple carriers is time-consuming and error-prone. Hidden roaming charges or inefficient usage policies eat into margins.<\/p>

3. Complex Cross-Border Deployments<\/strong>
In Africa, roaming agreements vary dramatically. A roaming SIM may connect in one country but fail in the next. Managing Steering, first-time installs,\u00a0 SIM swaps, troubleshooting, APN settings, and failover behaviour manually is unsustainable.<\/p>

4. Lack of Customer Confidence<\/strong>
When solution providers can\u2019t answer: \u201cWhy doesn\u2019t your SIM work?\u201d<\/em> they risk losing credibility. In enterprise IoT, trust is everything.<\/p>

Why a CMP is Essential for IoT Success<\/h2>

A Connectivity Management Portal is no longer a \u201cnice-to-have\u201d\u2014it\u2019s infrastructure. It ensures that IoT deployments are:<\/p>

  • Visible<\/strong>: every device, every session, every byte of data is tracked.<\/p><\/li>

  • Controllable<\/strong>: SIMs can be activated, suspended, or reconfigured in real time.<\/p><\/li>

  • Predictable<\/strong>: cost structures, data usage, and performance are transparent.<\/p><\/li>

  • Resilient<\/strong>: With multi-IMSI and multi-core network failover, downtime becomes the exception, not the rule.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

    Without a CMP, IoT providers are operating in the dark. With it, they gain command and control of their entire connectivity layer.<\/p>

    Inside the CommsCloud CMP (Powered by floLIVE)<\/h2>

    The CommsCloud CMP goes beyond \u201cbasic SIM management.\u201d It\u2019s built for the scale, complexity, and demands of global IoT providers.<\/p>

    Key Features<\/h3>

    • Real-Time Visibility<\/strong><\/p>

      • Live dashboards showing SIM status, network events, and data consumption.<\/p><\/li>

      • Signalling-level insights (e.g., location updates, PDP creation, error codes).<\/p><\/li>

      • Uniformity in event management no matter where in the world your device might find itself.\u00a0<\/li><\/ul><\/li>

      • Advanced SIM Lifecycle Management<\/strong><\/p>

        • Activate, suspend, or terminate SIMs instantly.<\/p><\/li>

        • OTA control of multi-IMSI profiles with autonomous switching.<\/p><\/li><\/ul><\/li>

        • Network Intelligence<\/strong><\/p>

          • Identify registration failures, poor signal environments, or misconfigured devices\/modems.<\/p><\/li>

          • Proactive alerts prevent downtime before customers notice.<\/p><\/li><\/ul><\/li>

          • Billing & Policy Control<\/strong><\/p>

            • Flexible rate plans across IMSI profiles.<\/p><\/li>

            • Set usage thresholds and prevent bill shock.<\/p><\/li><\/ul><\/li>

            • Security & Compliance<\/strong><\/p>

              • Private APN options for enterprises.<\/p><\/li>

              • Data sovereignty alignment with African regulations.<\/p><\/li><\/ul><\/li><\/ul>

                Business Benefits<\/h3>

                • Operational Efficiency<\/strong>: Automates manual processes, freeing teams to focus on growth.<\/p><\/li>

                • Customer Retention<\/strong>: Faster diagnosis and resolution builds client trust.<\/p><\/li>

                • Revenue Protection<\/strong>: Avoids unnecessary costs from roaming, misconfigurations, or downtime.<\/p><\/li>

                • Scalability<\/strong>: From 100 to 100,000 devices, the CMP ensures deployments remain manageable.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

                  Why Choose CommsCloud\u2019s CMP?<\/h2>

                  Unlike generic platforms, the CommsCloud CMP is:<\/p>

                  • Built for IoT, Global in Reach<\/strong>: Built to handle fragmented networks and unpredictable roaming environments.<\/p><\/li>

                  • Integrated to floLIVE\u2019s Cloud Native Core Infrastructure<\/strong>: Ensuring local breakout across five continents, regulatory compliance, and unmatched resilience.<\/p><\/li>

                  • Part of the Cloud Connect Ecosystem<\/strong>: Seamlessly integrated with CommsCloud Cloud Connect SIMs, allowing for private APNs, steering and enterprise-grade support.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

                    Conclusion: Turning IoT Chaos into Clarity<\/h2>

                    IoT solution providers cannot afford blind spots, downtime, or reactive troubleshooting. Our Connectivity Management Portal<\/strong> transforms connectivity management from a pain point into a strategic advantage.<\/p>

                    By delivering visibility, control, and resilience at scale, the CMP ensures your IoT deployments don\u2019t just survive Africa\u2019s complexity\u2014they thrive.<\/p>

                    \ud83d\udc49 Explore how CommsCloud\u2019s CMP can transform your connectivity today. [Contact Us]<\/p>","pa_condition_repeater":[],"_css_classes":"posts-p","custom_css":".posts-p p{\n margin: 5px\n}"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":false}],"isInner":false}]

A Single SKU. On one SIM. Infinite Possibilities<\/h2>

Every fleet manager, OEM, or security provider operating in Africa knows the pain: roaming SIMs that fail at borders, inflated data bills, and devices going offline at critical moments. Whether it\u2019s a dashcam, a push-to-talk radio, or a point-of-sale terminal, reliable IoT connectivity in Africa<\/strong> has always been difficult to scale.<\/p>

That\u2019s why CommsCloud developed the Cloud Connect SIM<\/strong> \u2014 a single SKU IoT SIM card<\/strong> built for Africa\u2019s realities. With one SIM, you gain multi-network, cross-border coverage, autonomous switching, and transparent pricing. It\u2019s not just a SIM; it\u2019s the backbone of your business uptime and it\u2019s a gateway to unmatched coverage, flexibility, and control, all powered by floLIVE\u2019s advanced, cloud-native IoT infrastructure<\/strong><\/a>.<\/p>

Why Traditional IoT SIMs Fail in Africa<\/h2>

  • Dead zones & dropped networks<\/strong>: Roaming SIMs only cover a limited number of mobile networks, creating blind spots when assets move across countries.<\/p><\/li>

  • Roaming costs that crush margins<\/strong>: High-data deployments like in-vehicle cameras or PTT radios see costs soar 50\u201370% under roaming.<\/p><\/li>

  • Complex installs & misconfigurations<\/strong>: Too often, devices are deployed incorrectly in the field, leaving operators wondering \u201cwhy doesn\u2019t the SIM work?\u201d when the issue is firmware or APN setup.<\/p><\/li>

  • No local resilience<\/strong>: When roaming agreements change or networks fail, devices can remain offline for weeks.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

    Africa\u2019s scale and complexity demand a SIM designed from the ground up \u2014 not a retrofitted roaming solution.<\/p>

    Single SKU SIM: One SIM, All Networks<\/h2>

    The CommsCloud Cloud Connect SIM<\/strong> solves Africa\u2019s toughest connectivity challenges:<\/p>

    • One SIM, Multiple Networks<\/strong> \u2013 Auto-switches to the strongest local operator.<\/p><\/li>

    • Cross-Border Ready<\/strong> \u2013 Seamless handovers from Zambia to South Africa, or JHB to DR Congo and beyond.\u00a0<\/p><\/li>

    • Always-On Resilience<\/strong> \u2013 Zero-downtime failover ensures cameras continue to stream and radios remain live.<\/p><\/li>

    • OEM-Friendly<\/strong> \u2013 eUICC, iSIM, or plastic SIM \u2014 one SKU, no integration chaos.<\/p><\/li>

    • Future-Proofed<\/strong> \u2013 Local breakout PGWs, LPWA compliant, and 5G ready.<\/p><\/li>

    • Smart Support<\/strong> \u2013 IoT-savvy experts who understand both SIM and device stacks.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

      SIM Applet Management for Intelligent Connectivity<\/h2>

      At the heart of this solution lies floLIVE\u2019s multi-IMSI SIM applet technology<\/strong>. Each SIM can hold and manage up to 10 IMSI profiles, enabling:<\/p>

      • Autonomous Switching<\/strong> \u2013 The SIM decides the best IMSI based on coverage, quality, or commercial rules.<\/p><\/li>

      • Firmware-Integrated Reliability<\/strong> \u2013 Working hand-in-hand with OEMs to embed the right settings directly into the device.<\/p><\/li>

      • Future-Proof Flexibility<\/strong> \u2013 Profiles can be added or reconfigured OTA (over-the-air) without field visits.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

        The result? Smarter devices that self-heal connectivity issues and adapt dynamically to local conditions.<\/p>

        Remote SIM Provisioning (RSP): Ready for Any Form Factor<\/h2>

        The future of IoT is eSIM and iSIM. CommsCloud supports Remote SIM Provisioning (RSP)<\/strong> across all form factors, giving enterprises the freedom to:<\/p>

        • Deploy Globally, Manage Locally<\/strong> \u2013 Download new operator profiles remotely, without requiring device access.<\/p><\/li>

        • Reduce Logistics Costs<\/strong> \u2013 No need to stockpile multiple SIM SKUs.<\/p><\/li>

          This ensures enterprises are ready not only for today\u2019s deployments but also for tomorrow\u2019s growth.<\/p>

          CommsCloud selected floLIVE as our partner for one simple reason: they own the technology stack end-to-end<\/strong>\u2014from the SIM to the cloud.<\/p>

At CommsCloud, we understand that IoT connectivity is mission-critical<\/strong>\u2014devices can\u2019t afford downtime, and businesses need the assurance that their provider is standing behind every SIM, every session, every connection. Our Service Level Agreement (SLA) is designed to give clients confidence in the quality, reliability, and responsiveness<\/strong> of our services.<\/p>

1. Service Delivery Commitments<\/h2>

What We Commit To<\/strong><\/p>

  • High Availability<\/strong>: CommsCloud commits to delivering network and platform uptime of 99.9% or higher<\/strong> across our Cloud Connect SIM ecosystem, powered by floLIVE\u2019s cloud-native core networks.<\/p><\/li>

  • Resilient Connectivity<\/strong>: Multi-IMSI and multi-core network failover built into every SIM, ensuring continuous service even when a carrier network experiences outages.<\/p><\/li>

    • Predictable performance across borders and networks.<\/p><\/li>

    • Reduced operational risk with built-in redundancy.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

      2. Response Times<\/h2>

      What We Commit To<\/strong><\/p>

      • Critical Issues (Severity 1)<\/strong> \u2013 Connectivity failures affecting entire fleets or critical services: Initial response within 1 hour<\/strong>, resolution within defined escalation protocols.<\/p><\/li>

      • High-Priority Issues (Severity 2)<\/strong> \u2013 Service interruptions affecting a subset of devices or major performance degradation: Initial response within 2 hours<\/strong>, target resolution or workaround within 8 business hours.<\/p><\/li>

      • Standard Issues (Severity 3)<\/strong> \u2013 Non-urgent technical issues or queries: Response within 1 business day<\/strong>, resolution within 3\u20135 business days.<\/p><\/li>

      • Service Requests (Severity 4)<\/strong> \u2013 Routine provisioning, configuration, or information requests: Response within 2 business days<\/strong>.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

        Client Benefit<\/strong><\/p>

        • Clear expectations on how quickly issues will be addressed.<\/p><\/li>

        • Confidence that business-critical incidents will be prioritised and escalated with urgency.<\/p><\/li>

        • Faster time to resolution, reducing downtime and disruption.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

          3. Support Availability<\/h2>

          What We Commit To<\/strong><\/p>

          • Standard Support<\/strong>: Available during business hours (Monday\u2013Friday, 08:00\u201317:00 SAST).<\/p><\/li>

          • 24\/7 Critical Support<\/strong>: For Severity 1 incidents, CommsCloud provides round-the-clock support coverage<\/strong>, including weekends and holidays.<\/p><\/li>

          • Multi-Channel Access<\/strong>: Clients can access support via email, portal (JIRA integration), and WhatsApp handovers, with automatic ticket creation for traceability.<\/p><\/li>

          • Proactive Monitoring<\/strong>: Network and device signalling are monitored continuously, enabling early detection and intervention.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

            Client Benefit<\/strong><\/p>

            • Assurance that urgent issues are handled no matter when they occur.<\/p><\/li>

            • Convenient access to support channels that suit client workflows.<\/p><\/li>

            • Proactive resolution of issues before they become customer-visible problems.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

              4. Escalation Process<\/h2>

              What We Commit To<\/strong><\/p>

              • Tiered escalation through support, operations, and technical leadership.<\/p><\/li>

              • Dedicated escalation manager assigned to Severity 1 issues.<\/p><\/li>

              • Regular status updates are provided until resolution is achieved.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

                Client Benefit<\/strong><\/p>

                • Structured accountability across CommsCloud teams.<\/p><\/li>

                • Transparency during incidents with clear communication.<\/p><\/li>

                • Confidence that issues will not stall at lower tiers.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

                  5. Service Quality & Reliability<\/h2>

                  What We Commit To<\/strong><\/p>

                  • Regular reporting on SIM activity, uptime, and incident metrics.<\/p><\/li>

                  • SLA reviews are aligned with the client's business needs.<\/p><\/li>

                  • Continuous improvement of processes and infrastructure.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

                    Client Benefit<\/strong><\/p>

                    • Visibility into service quality through measurable metrics.<\/p><\/li>

                    • A partner committed to evolving with your business.<\/p><\/li>

                    • Long-term confidence that CommsCloud is invested in your success.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

                      In Closing<\/h2>

                      • Reliable, measurable service quality<\/strong> that underpins business-critical IoT operations.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

                        With CommsCloud, you don\u2019t just get IoT connectivity\u2014you get the confidence that your deployments will run with the service quality and reliability<\/strong> enterprises demand.<\/p>","pa_condition_repeater":[],"_css_classes":"posts-p","custom_css":".posts-p p{\n margin: 5px\n}"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":false}],"isInner":false}]

title: What Are the Best IoT Connectivity Solutions for African Businesses?
author: peter.walsh@commscloud.com
excerpt: What are the best IoT connectivity solutions for businesses? This article provides an in-depth exploration of various connection methods tailored for enterprises.
content_text: A Practical Guide to Real Uptime, Real ROI Selecting the right IoT connectivity solution can make or break your deployment — especially in Africa, where networks vary wildly between cities, corridors, and borders. For fleets, OEMs, and integrators, the question isn’t just about technology; it’s about reliability, visibility, and cost control. At CommsCloud, we’ve seen what happens when the wrong SIM meets the wrong network — blackouts at borders, roaming bill shock, and devices that vanish from dashboards. Here’s how to avoid that, and what connectivity options actually deliver uptime where it matters most. LPWAN, NB-IoT, LTE-M — Which One Works for Africa? Low Power Wide Area Networks (LPWAN) such as NB-IoT and LoRaWAN promise long-range coverage with minimal power use — perfect for sensors in agriculture, utilities, and infrastructure. [{"id":"2b5efb60","elType":"section","settings":{"pa_condition_repeater":[]},"elements":[{"id":"51ca3e69","elType":"column","settings":{"_column_size":100,"pa_condition_repeater":[]},"elements":[{"id":"429983fb","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

A Practical Guide to Real Uptime, Real ROI<\/h3>

Selecting the right IoT connectivity solution can make or break your deployment \u2014 especially in Africa, where networks vary wildly between cities, corridors, and borders. For fleets, OEMs, and integrators, the question isn\u2019t just<\/em> about technology; it\u2019s about reliability, visibility, and cost control.<\/p>

At CommsCloud, we\u2019ve seen what happens when the wrong SIM meets the wrong network \u2014 blackouts at borders, roaming bill shock, and devices that vanish from dashboards. Here\u2019s how to avoid that, and what connectivity options actually deliver uptime where it matters most.<\/p>

LPWAN, NB-IoT, LTE-M \u2014 Which One Works for Africa?<\/h2>

Low Power Wide Area Networks (LPWAN) such as NB-IoT and LoRaWAN promise long-range coverage with minimal power use \u2014 perfect for sensors in agriculture, utilities, and infrastructure.
But in practice, these networks are still maturing across Africa<\/strong>. While NB-IoT is available in major metros like Johannesburg and Nairobi, coverage beyond city limits can be patchy.<\/p>

That\u2019s why CommsCloud\u2019s Cloud Connect SIMs<\/strong><\/a> support all major technologies \u2014 2G, 3G, 4G, LTE-M, and NB-IoT \u2014 on a single card. Devices connect autonomously to the strongest local network, so even if NB-IoT isn\u2019t available, your data still flows over LTE or GSM without manual intervention.<\/p>

\u2705 Best For:<\/strong> Utilities, smart metering, and agricultural sensors where devices need long battery life and wide reach.
\u2705 CommsCloud Advantage:<\/strong> Multi-IMSI SIMs with built-in failover \u2014 one SIM, all borders, no downtime.<\/p>

Multi-IMSI | Multi Network SIMs: Africa\u2019s Real Game-Changer<\/h2>

Traditional roaming SIMs might work for a week \u2014 until they don\u2019t. Commercial roaming agreements shift, networks drop, and your \u201cglobal\u201d IoT project goes dark.<\/p>

That\u2019s why Cloud Connect SIMs are different. They use multiple IMSI profiles across multiple core networks<\/strong>, each with its own commercial relationship and local breakout. The result?<\/p>

  • Autonomous switching<\/strong> between networks when coverage fails.<\/p><\/li>

  • Local routing<\/strong> for lower latency and better compliance.<\/p><\/li>

  • No roaming costs<\/strong> \u2014 ever.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

    This architecture is tailor-made for Africa\u2019s realities: long routes, variable towers, and zero tolerance for downtime.<\/p>

    \u2705 Best For:<\/strong> Cross-border fleets, PTT radios, and dashcams that demand always-on connectivity.
    \u2705 CommsCloud Advantage:<\/strong> Intelligent applet switching powered by floLIVE \u2014 not your device firmware \u2014 so uptime is managed from the SIM itself.<\/p>

    Scaling IoT Without the Chaos<\/h2>

    Every business hits a scaling wall eventually \u2014 too many SIMs, too little visibility.
    To scale IoT infrastructure effectively, you need centralized control, not fragmented portals.<\/p>

    CommsCloud\u2019s Connectivity Management Platform (CMP)<\/strong><\/a> lets you:<\/p>

    • Activate, suspend, or reassign SIMs in seconds.<\/p><\/li>

    • Monitor usage and network health in real time.<\/p><\/li>

    • Diagnose issues remotely \u2014 no site visits needed.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

      When paired with proactive device settings validation (our OEM Settings Library<\/strong>), clients reduce field faults by over 30%. Because resilience isn\u2019t an add-on \u2014 it\u2019s engineered from the start.<\/p>

      \u2705 Best For:<\/strong> OEMs and system integrators deploying hundreds or thousands of devices.
      \u2705 CommsCloud Advantage:<\/strong> A single dashboard for every SIM, every country, every network.<\/p>

      Securing Your IoT Data End-to-End<\/h2>

      IoT deployments in logistics, security, and utilities can\u2019t afford exposure.
      That\u2019s why CommsCloud offers Private APNs and VPNs<\/strong> to create fully isolated data paths between devices and cloud systems.<\/p>

      This means:<\/p>

      • End-to-end encryption<\/strong> for sensitive telemetry.<\/p><\/li>

      • IP whitelisting<\/strong> to lock down access by device or server.<\/p><\/li>

      • No dependency on public internet routing<\/strong>, minimizing latency and risk.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

        \u2705 Best For:<\/strong> Video surveillance, financial POS, and smart infrastructure.
        \u2705 CommsCloud Advantage:<\/strong> Secure, compliant connectivity designed for African data sovereignty standards.<\/p>

        Cost-Effective Connectivity That Scales With You<\/h2>

        Small businesses often think IoT is out of reach \u2014 but it doesn\u2019t have to be.
        Our pay-as-you-grow model<\/strong> and flexible data tiers (from 300 MB for telemetry to multi-gigabyte dashcam bundles) let you scale without lock-ins or roaming fees.<\/p>

        You only pay for what you use \u2014 and every SIM is backed by real humans<\/strong> who understand your devices and terrain, not offshore call centres.<\/p>

        \u2705 Best For:<\/strong> SMEs and fleets expanding regionally.
        \u2705 CommsCloud Advantage:<\/strong> Month-to-month plans with no hidden costs, built for African CFOs who value predictability over promises.<\/p>

        Finding the best<\/em> IoT connectivity solution isn\u2019t about buzzwords. It\u2019s about uptime, visibility, and trust.

        Here\u2019s what works in Africa today:<\/p>

        Connectivity Type<\/th>Ideal Use Case<\/th>Key Limitation<\/th>Cloud Connect Edge<\/th><\/tr><\/thead>
        LPWAN \/ NB-IoT<\/strong><\/td>Low-power sensors<\/td>Patchy coverage<\/td>Multi-tech fallback<\/td><\/tr>
        LTE-M \/ 4G<\/strong><\/td>Fleets & logistics<\/td>Variable latency<\/td>Local breakout<\/td><\/tr>
        Roaming SIMs<\/strong><\/td>Global pilots<\/td>High cost, blackouts<\/td>Local IMSIs, no roaming<\/td><\/tr>
        Cloud Connect SIMs<\/strong><\/td>Cross-border, high-data<\/td>None \u2014 built for Africa<\/td>Always-On, multi-core resilience<\/td><\/tr><\/tbody><\/table><\/div><\/div>

        With Cloud Connect SIMs<\/strong>, you\u2019re not choosing between speed, cost, or control \u2014 you get all three. From Lusaka to Durban, Nairobi to Gaborone, your devices stay online. No roaming. No downtime. Just resilience in motion.<\/p>

        [wpu_silo]<\/p>","pa_condition_repeater":[]},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":false}],"isInner":false}]

title: Embedded SIM: How It Works, Pros/Cons, and 5 Best Practices
author: peter.walsh@commscloud.com
excerpt:
The terms “embedded SIM” and “eSIM” are often used interchangeably, but they mean very different things. 

For anyone deploying IoT at scale, understanding the distinction can determine whether your devices remain flexible, compliant, and cost-efficient over their lifetime.
content_text: August 26 | By Amit Elimelech - edited and adapted for CommsCloud by Peter Walsh. What Is an Embedded SIM? An embedded SIM (eSIM or soldered SIM) is a SIM chip that’s built directly into a device’s motherboard, eliminating the need for a removable SIM card. Instead of being inserted manually, it’s integrated at the manufacturing stage, improving durability, saving space, and simplifying design. Embedded SIMs are now common across smartphones, tablets, wearables, connected vehicles, and IoT devices , where long-term reliability and remote connectivity are essential. It’s worth noting: while people often use embedded SIM and eSIM interchangeably, they aren’t quite the same. Embedded SIM describes the form factor —a SIM that’s permanently built into the device. eSIM (with eUICC) describes the capability —a SIM that supports remote provisioning , letting users switch carriers or profiles digitally, without replacing hardware. Many embedded SIMs include eUICC functionality, but not all. Som

meta._elementor_data:
[{"id":"cc9f8e6","elType":"section","settings":{"pa_condition_repeater":[]},"elements":[{"id":"f13f5b9","elType":"column","settings":{"_column_size":100,"_inline_size":null,"pa_condition_repeater":[]},"elements":[{"id":"ba86a55","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

August 26 | By Amit Elimelech - edited and adapted for CommsCloud by Peter Walsh.<\/em><\/p>

What Is an Embedded SIM?<\/h2>

An embedded SIM (eSIM or soldered SIM)<\/strong> is a SIM chip that\u2019s built directly into a device\u2019s motherboard, eliminating the need for a removable SIM card. Instead of being inserted manually, it\u2019s integrated at the manufacturing stage, improving durability, saving space, and simplifying design.<\/p>

Embedded SIMs are now common across smartphones, tablets, wearables, connected vehicles, and IoT devices<\/strong>, where long-term reliability and remote connectivity are essential.<\/p>

It\u2019s worth noting: while people often use embedded SIM<\/em> and eSIM<\/em> interchangeably, they aren\u2019t quite the same.<\/p>

  • Embedded SIM<\/strong> describes the form factor<\/em>\u2014a SIM that\u2019s permanently built into the device.<\/p><\/li>

  • eSIM (with eUICC)<\/strong> describes the capability<\/em>\u2014a SIM that supports remote provisioning<\/strong>, letting users switch carriers or profiles digitally, without replacing hardware.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

    Many embedded SIMs include eUICC functionality, but not all. Some are fixed to a single carrier and can\u2019t be updated remotely.<\/p>

    How Embedded SIM Technology Works<\/h2>

    An embedded SIM is a tiny microchip soldered onto a device\u2019s motherboard. Functionally, it\u2019s identical to a traditional SIM\u2014it authenticates the device on mobile networks and stores subscriber data\u2014but with a different physical design.<\/p>

    If an embedded SIM includes eUICC<\/strong> capabilities, carriers can remotely provision or update network profiles. This makes it ideal for connected ecosystems like smart cities, industrial IoT, and global enterprise deployments, where devices may be distributed across multiple networks and regions.<\/p>

    By removing the need for SIM trays and plastic cards, manufacturers gain more design freedom<\/strong>, allowing for thinner builds, improved water resistance, and larger batteries\u2014all while maintaining secure, reliable connectivity.<\/p>

    Embedded SIM vs. eSIM: Key Differences<\/h2>
    Feature<\/strong><\/th>Embedded SIM (without eUICC)<\/strong><\/th>eSIM (with eUICC)<\/strong><\/th><\/tr><\/thead>
    Physical Form<\/td>Soldered onto motherboard<\/td>Can be embedded or removable<\/td><\/tr>
    Profile Management<\/td>One fixed mobile profile<\/td>Multiple profiles, remotely switchable<\/td><\/tr>
    Remote Provisioning<\/td>Not supported<\/td>Fully supported<\/td><\/tr>
    Typical Use Cases<\/td>Basic IoT, older consumer devices<\/td>Smartphones, enterprise IoT, automotive<\/td><\/tr>
    Flexibility<\/td>Locked to one carrier<\/td>Global, multi-network ready<\/td><\/tr><\/tbody><\/table><\/div><\/div>

    In short:<\/strong> all eSIMs are embedded SIMs, but not all embedded SIMs are eSIMs.<\/p>

    Embedded SIM vs. Traditional SIM Cards<\/h2>

    Both technologies connect devices to mobile networks, but their designs differ fundamentally.<\/p>

    Traditional SIM cards<\/strong> are removable. Embedded SIMs<\/strong> are permanently built in.
    Here\u2019s how they compare:<\/p>

    • Space and durability:<\/strong> Embedded SIMs free up physical space and remove mechanical points of failure.<\/p><\/li>

    • Security:<\/strong> Embedded SIMs can\u2019t be easily removed or cloned.<\/p><\/li>

    • Device management:<\/strong> Remote provisioning enables over-the-air updates for thousands of devices.<\/p><\/li>

    • Compatibility:<\/strong> While nearly all carriers support physical SIMs, eSIM and embedded SIM adoption is still expanding globally.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

      Pros and Cons of Embedded SIMs<\/h2>

      Advantages<\/h3>

      • Space-saving design:<\/strong> Enables sleeker devices with better battery and waterproofing.<\/p><\/li>

      • Enhanced security:<\/strong> Non-removable SIMs reduce theft and tampering risks.<\/p><\/li>

      • Greater durability:<\/strong> Resistant to dust, water, and physical wear.<\/p><\/li>

      • Efficient management:<\/strong> Streamlines device onboarding and carrier changes for IoT fleets.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

        Limitations<\/h3>

        • Carrier lock-in:<\/strong> Some embedded SIMs can\u2019t switch profiles.<\/p><\/li>

        • No quick swapping:<\/strong> Requires software provisioning instead of manual replacement.<\/p><\/li>

        • Compatibility gaps:<\/strong> Not all devices\u2014or regions\u2014fully support eUICC provisioning yet.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

          Devices That Support Embedded SIMs<\/h2>

          The technology is now standard across many device categories:<\/p>

          • Smartphones:<\/strong> Apple, Samsung, and Google flagship models<\/p><\/li>

          • Tablets:<\/strong> iPad Pro, Galaxy Tab<\/p><\/li>

          • Wearables:<\/strong> Apple Watch, Galaxy Watch<\/p><\/li>

          • Laptops:<\/strong> Surface, HP, Lenovo<\/p><\/li>

          • IoT & Automotive:<\/strong> Connected cars, industrial sensors, fleet systems<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

            As carrier support expands, embedded SIMs will become the default choice for most connected devices.<\/p>

            The Next Step: iSIM (Integrated SIM)<\/h2>

            iSIM<\/strong> represents the next evolution\u2014embedding SIM functionality directly into the main processor or system-on-chip (SoC).<\/p>

            Feature<\/strong><\/th>Embedded SIM<\/strong><\/th>iSIM<\/strong><\/th><\/tr><\/thead>
            Placement<\/td>Soldered on motherboard<\/td>Built into processor<\/td><\/tr>
            Space Efficiency<\/td>Eliminates SIM tray<\/td>Even smaller footprint<\/td><\/tr>
            Power Use<\/td>Separate power management<\/td>More efficient<\/td><\/tr>
            Security<\/td>High<\/td>Enhanced (hardware-level)<\/td><\/tr>
            Adoption<\/td>Mainstream<\/td>Emerging (especially in IoT)<\/td><\/tr><\/tbody><\/table><\/div><\/div>

            Benefits of iSIMs<\/strong> include improved energy efficiency, reduced production costs, and stronger tamper resistance. However, widespread carrier and device support are still developing.<\/p>

            4 Best Practices for Implementing Embedded SIM Technology<\/h2>

            1. Optimize Device Design<\/h3>

            Eliminate the SIM tray to save space for improved thermal management, waterproofing, and antenna performance.<\/p>

            2. Use for IoT and Enterprise Deployments<\/h3>

            Leverage eUICC<\/strong> to simplify remote provisioning, support multi-carrier access, and reduce downtime for distributed IoT networks.<\/p>

            3. Manage Multiple Profiles Effectively<\/h3>

            For devices supporting multiple carrier profiles, integrate the Local Profile Assistant (LPA)<\/strong> to handle installation and activation securely.<\/p>

            4. Ensure Compliance and Security<\/h3>

            Adhere to GSMA eSIM specifications<\/strong> <\/a>and regional telecom regulations. Use encryption and authentication to protect devices from unauthorized access.<\/p>

            Managing Embedded SIMs at Scale<\/h2>

            Platforms like floLIVE\u2019s Connectivity Management Platform<\/a> (CMP)<\/strong> integrate eUICC services<\/strong> directly, offering centralized provisioning for both M2M and Consumer<\/strong> eSIMs. Their globally distributed, GSMA-accredited data centers<\/a> (Austin and London) ensure secure, scalable, and compliant connectivity management for enterprise and IoT deployments.<\/p>

            Key Benefits<\/h2>

            • Reduces production and logistics costs<\/p><\/li>

            • Enables high-data applications and multi-region use<\/p><\/li>

            • Simplifies activation (QR or remote)<\/p><\/li>

            • Supports profile transfers and automated provisioning<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

              For organizations managing large fleets of connected devices, this unified approach streamlines operations and improves agility.<\/p>

              Final Thoughts<\/h2>

              Embedded SIMs\u2014and their successors, eSIMs and iSIMs\u2014are transforming how devices connect to networks. By removing physical barriers and enabling global, digital connectivity, they pave the way for smarter, more scalable connected ecosystems.<\/p>

              [wpu_silo]<\/p>","pa_condition_repeater":[]},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":false}],"isInner":false}]
